objective: Architectural sign-off on the mostConnectedNode pure helper and carto stats CLI extension for CART-S02-T03
key_changes: mostConnectedNode() pure helper added to graph.ts — counts from/to incidences, returns { node: Node | null, degree: number }
carto stats CLI extended to print most-connected node title and degree (or (no edges) sentinel)
4 vitest tests added for mostConnectedNode: no-edges sentinel, bidirectional degree, first-encountered tie-break, empty graph
findings: mostConnectedNode is a pure export in graph.ts — no I/O side effects — consistent with the project's architecture boundary
No data model or persistence changes — purely additive extension to existing CLI output
Tie-break semantics are deterministic (graph.nodes order) and test-covered
Dangling edge references are silently skipped; consistent with project's stance on referential integrity
Minor UX edge case: all-dangling edges produces 'N edges' on line 1 but '(no edges)' on line 2 — advisory only
graphStats() lost its 2 unit tests — zero coverage on a trivial function; recommend restoring in hygiene pass
No cross-module conflicts, no breaking changes, no deployment or migration impact
Build, lint, and all 23 tests pass cleanly
